name: Musmehlmus (Whole Wheat Mush)
recipeIngredient: 200 grams Whole Wheat Flour (7 ounces) (a coarsely ground wheat flour, from which only part of the bran has been removed)
recipeInstructions:
1. From grandmother's more thrifty times; rarely encountered today.
2. Bring the milk to a boil. Stirring constantly, add the whole wheat flour. Keep at a low boil for 10 to 15 minutes, stirring constantly.
3. When ready to serve, carefully pour the melted butter over the mush (which should already have formed a skin), and place the rye bread cubes on top.
4. The mush tastes best when eaten from a copper skillet, and must have a browned crust on the bottom.
5. Serves 4.
From: D'SCHWAEBISCH' KUCHE' by Aegidius Kolb and Leonhard Lidel, Allgaeuer Zeitungsverlag, Kempten. 1976. (Translation/Conversion: Karin Brewer)
